body:
TUNGSTEN "keys and smith" pass complete for this session -- SLS(68) -> SLS(72)/9.86.

REAL FINDING: WARRANT#54-64 (3 separate prior passes -- forge-cleanup-3 seam audit, forge-parallel Sharpe/regimeWR, keys-3 readability) were all shipped under the SAME stale SLS(68) identity -- EA_BUILD_ID and forge_ledger.json were never bumped/updated across any of them. Source itself was fine; the ledger/version banner had drifted from reality. Backfilled the ledger (pass_42-44) and reconciled identity to SLS(69).

Shipped 3 new bounded warrants this pass, each compiled 0 errors and diff-verified:
- WARRANT#65 (keys-3, item 15 partial): Keys3_EpisodeState -- readability lifecycle classifier (noise/emerging/coherent/dissipating), pure/uncalled -> SLS(70)
- WARRANT#66 (forge-indicate, r4 remainder): empirical perfect-buy/sell envelope diagnostic, wired into ghost-validation reporting -> SLS(71)
- WARRANT#67 (forge-edge-2, r7 "per-regime ghost power"): fixed an underpowered-regime-regression gate in the ratchet's non-inferiority check (same defect class as WARRANT#12/13/14/50/51) -> SLS(72)

Audited WARRANT#54-64 by direct read (not exhaustive) -- no new defects beyond the ledger drift above.

NOT claiming "fullproof" -- Keys-3's R has never been validated against a real run (both live attach attempts hit the still-unexplained "Terminal closing" issue), PBO is still a stub, and forge-edge-2's Convergence-Sigma / forge-indicate's reliability-curve remain real, larger, un-shipped work. Attaching the final SLS(72) .mq4 + .ex4 and the updated forge_ledger.json.
